hooks.prompt: The user is about to run a git commit. Check whether CLAUDE.md needs to be updated to reflect the staged changes. Steps: 1) Read the current CLAUDE.md. 2) Run 'git diff --cached --name-only' to see which files are staged for commit. 3) If any staged changes affect architecture, commands, dependencies, or conventions documented in CLAUDE.md, respond with exactly this JSON: {"continue": false, "stopReason": "CLAUDE.md needs to be updated to reflect the staged changes. Please update CLAUDE.md before committing."} 4) If CLAUDE.md is already up to date, respond with exactly: {"continue": true}

The user is about to run a command that may involve solving problems or modifying the project. Check whether the user should review the BASECASE.md file first. Steps: 1) Read the current BASECASE.md file. 2) Check if there are any critical basecase items that may not be valid for the current operation. 3) If the user should review the basecase first, respond with exactly this JSON: {"continue": false, "stopReason": "Before solving problems, please review the BASECASE.md file to ensure all basecase conditions are valid."} 4) If the basecase is already valid for the current operation, respond with exactly: {"continue": true}

The user is about to launch an agent to solve a problem. Check whether the user should review the BASECASE.md file first. Steps: 1) Read the current BASECASE.md file. 2) Check if there are any critical basecase items that may not be valid for the current task. 3) If the user should review the basecase first, respond with exactly this JSON: {"continue": false, "stopReason": "Before solving problems, please review the BASECASE.md file to ensure all basecase conditions are valid."} 4) If the basecase is already valid for the current task, respond with exactly: {"continue": true}
